text: Title: Advocating for Humanity: Abraham's Prayer for Sodom (Genesis 18:16-33) and the Pentecostal Experience of Faith This article, by means of qualitative methodology employing narrative interpretation and an in-depth literature review of Genesis 18:16-33, addresses the theme of Abraham's prayer for Sodom and explores its theological implications in relation to the Pentecostal experience of faith. The research delves into Abraham's life of faithful surrender to God, together with his prayer for Sodom in an attempt to investigate its relevance and contribution to the believer's walk of faith. The findings of the study indicate that Abraham's prayer for Sodom has a seminal function in the life of the believer within the context of the Pentecostal experience of faith, imparting a number of values and responsibilities such as faithful perseverance in prayer, undertaking courageous intercession for the sake of humanity, and acknowledging the role of the Holy Spirit in equipping the church for effective outreach and evangelism. In this respect, the story of Abraham's prayer in Genesis 18:16-33 is seen as a perpetual model of "spiritual activism" in which intercessory prayer plays a pivotal role, thus facilitating a lasting interface between biblical narrative and contemporary experience of faith among Penteces.
